title: Timeout Layers, Deadlines, Cancellation, Cleanup, and Budget Allocation · Browser and Node Client… · Compiler Case: Phase Budget Allocation
summary: A search-as-you-type interface cancels stale requests with composed AbortSignals while preserving the winning result and announced status. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts phase budget allocation increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch end-to-end request budget and cancellation model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Allocate an end-to-end latency budget across attempts and phases while preserving time for cleanup and fallback. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Propagate caller cancellation and deadlines through request creation, helpers, parsing, retries, and downstream work. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Predict Phase Budget Allocation · Theory Phase Budget Allocation
steps.instruction:
theory case — TypeScript Fetch evidence model has 600 ms end-to-end for DNS, connect, TLS, headers, streamed body, parse, and cleanup. A caller cancels during body read; allocate phase budgets, propagate the reason, stop owned work, and preserve unknown remote effect. Competency probe: Allocate an end-to-end latency budget across attempts and phases while preserving time for cleanup and fallback. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
